name: Knack Packaging
about: Ahmedabad-based integrated packaging solutions provider manufacturing PLWPP bags for food, pet food, agrochemical and construction sectors; exports to 68+ countries.
strengths: 68+ country export footprint
Integrated manufacturing (backward-linked)
Diversified end-user base
risks: Raw material (polypropylene) price volatility
Customer concentration in a few large accounts
Working-capital intensive model

name: IC Electricals
about: Manufactures electronic equipment for Indian Railways (ERRUs, vigilance control devices, passenger info systems) under a B2G model, plus railway electrification EPC work.
strengths: B2G model with Indian Railways
Growing electrification order book
Niche technical certification moat
risks: Concentrated government-client dependency
Tender-based revenue is lumpy
Working capital tied up in receivables

name: SBI Funds Management
about: India's largest asset management company by mutual fund quarterly average AUM (~₹29.46 lakh crore as of Mar 2026), and investment manager to SBI Mutual Fund — a joint venture between State Bank of India and Amundi. 100% offer-for-sale; the company receives no proceeds from the IPO.
strengths: India's largest AMC by AUM
Strong brand trust (SBI + Amundi parentage)
Diversified fund product mix
risks: Pure OFS — no fresh capital to the company
AMC earnings sensitive to market cycles
Fee-compression pressure industry-wide

name: Kusumgar
about: Mumbai-based manufacturer of engineered synthetic fabrics (aerospace & defence, industrial, automotive, outdoor). IPO is entirely an offer for sale.
strengths: Aerospace & defence qualified supplier
High-margin specialty fabric mix
Decades-long promoter track record
risks: Entirely OFS — no fresh growth capital raised
Niche end markets limit volume scale
Import dependency for certain chemicals

name: Devson Catalyst
about: Gujarat-based manufacturer of catalysts, adsorbents and ceramic balls for refineries, petrochemical, steel and fertilizer producers; exports to 15+ countries.
strengths: High ROE (45.97%)
Import-substitution positioning
15+ country export base
risks: Small-cap SME liquidity risk
Customer concentration in refining sector
Raw material import dependency

name: Happy Steel
about: Ludhiana (Punjab) manufacturer of forged & precision-machined axles, spline shafts and spindles for automotive OEMs and Tier-1 suppliers.
strengths: OEM/Tier-1 supplier relationships
Precision forging capability
Export presence
risks: Cyclical auto-sector demand exposure
Fixed-price band signals modest demand so far
Raw steel price volatility

name: Kratikal Tech
about: AI-driven SaaS cybersecurity company (Threatcop, AutoSecT) serving BFSI, fintech, telecom and healthcare clients across India, UAE and USA. CERT-In empanelled.
strengths: CERT-In empanelled
SaaS recurring-revenue model
Multi-geography client base
risks: Intense competition from global cybersecurity vendors
Small revenue base vs valuation
Talent-retention risk in tech

name: Teja Engineering
about: Bharuch (Gujarat) engineering services firm across Oil & Gas, Power and Energy — O&M, erection & commissioning, calibration, overhaul/decommissioning.
strengths: Long-term O&M contracts
Diversified oil/gas/power clients
Asset-light services model
risks: Weak grey-market response pre-listing
Client concentration risk
Margins tied to contract renewals

name: Vinit Mobile
about: Surat-based multi-brand mobile phone retail chain with 35 company-owned stores across Gujarat and Rajasthan, plus selective B2B bulk supply.
strengths: Established multi-brand retail footprint
Diversified OEM relationships
Regional market density
risks: Severely undersubscribed issue (0.02x)
Thin retail margins
High competition from e-commerce

name: Sampark India Logistics
about: Carrying-and-forwarding agent offering freight forwarding, warehousing and distribution via 50 branch offices across 18 states and 8 leased warehouses.
strengths: Pan-India 50-branch network
Asset-light C&F model
Diversified client industries
risks: Thin net margins typical of logistics
Fuel-cost sensitivity
Fragmented, competitive sector

name: Atharva Polyplast
about: Satara (Maharashtra) manufacturer of precision injection-moulded plastic components for furniture, home-appliance and automotive OEMs / Tier-1 suppliers.
strengths: Precision moulding capability
OEM/Tier-1 relationships
High ROE (26.65%)
risks: Small-cap scale limits pricing power
Polymer input-cost volatility
Post-listing GMP has been softening

name: Seemax Resources
about: Vadodara-based rental & trading of material handling equipment (forklifts, cranes, pallet/reach trucks); ~95% revenue from recurring rentals.
strengths: ~95% recurring rental revenue
Very high ROE (48.65%)
Diversified industrial client base
risks: Tiny revenue base (₹14.46 Cr)
Capital-intensive fleet ownership
No GMP premium at listing

name: Aastha Spintex
about: Morbi (Gujarat) manufacturer of carded, combed and compact-combed cotton yarns from an integrated spinning-and-ginning facility; funds part-acquire Falcon Yarns.
strengths: Integrated spinning-ginning facility
Acquisition-funded capacity growth
Diversified yarn product mix
risks: Cotton price volatility
Highly competitive commodity textile space
Listing-day gain has compressed to near-flat

auditTrail.details: Imported during database migration

name: Adon Agro
about: Navi Mumbai sourcer/importer/distributor of dry fruits, nuts, seeds and berries — sold in bulk and under retail brand 'Hunger Nuts'.
strengths: Growing branded retail line (Hunger Nuts)
Diversified sourcing geography
Rising consumer demand for dry fruits
risks: Import/currency exposure
Undersubscribed issue (0.9x)
Low-margin commodity trading core

name: CSM Technologies
about: Bhubaneswar GovTech company, 27+ years building e-governance platforms for mining, agriculture, education and healthcare clients in India and Africa.
strengths: 27+ year GovTech track record
Multi-country (India + Africa) presence
Diversified government verticals
risks: Listed flat, then hit lower circuit (-5%)
Government-tender dependent revenue
Long sales cycles

name: Caliber Mining
about: Provides integrated mining services, coal extraction operations, and heavy bulk freight logistics solutions for power utilities and industrial producers.
strengths: Integrated end-to-end service offering
Long-term revenue visibility from utility contracts
High operating margin profile
risks: Highly dependent on government coal contracts
Capital intensive machinery fleet operations
Environmental regulations exposure

auditTrail.details: Imported during database migration

name: Cube Highways
about: Infrastructure Investment Trust (InvIT) focused on holding and operating a diversified portfolio of toll and annuity road assets across major national highway corridors in India.
strengths: Stable cash flows from long-term highway assets
High yield potential sponsored by global institutional investors
Well-maintained road portfolio
risks: Toll revenue risk from traffic volatility
Interest rate fluctuations impact debt servicing
Regulatory road construction risks

name: Sotefin Bharat
about: Designs, manufactures, and installs automated smart car parking systems, mechanical lifts, and multi-level parking solutions for premium real estate developers and municipal corporations.
strengths: Niche play in rapid urban traffic infrastructure
Proprietary design patents
Healthy order book from metropolitan hubs
risks: Dependent on real estate market cycles
High concentration of metropolitan projects
Raw steel price sensitivity

name: Cult.fit
about: India's largest health and fitness platform providing digital and offline services across cult.fit gyms, mental health services (mind.fit), and healthy food plans (eat.fit).
strengths: Leading consumer fitness brand in India
Highly scalable hybrid digital/offline model
Strong investor parentage
risks: Consistently high customer acquisition costs
Intense competition in regional gym sectors
Path to profitability is still early stage

auditTrail.details: Imported during database migration

name: Ratnadeep Retail
about: Operates a premium supermarket chain with over 150 stores in Southern India, offering fresh foods, groceries, and personal care products.
strengths: Strong regional brand density in South India
Robust supply chain and private label integration
Consistent cash-generative store operations
risks: Geographical concentration risk
Intense competition from online quick-commerce apps
High real estate leasing costs

auditTrail.details: Imported during database migration

name: Swara Baby Products
about: Manufactures hygiene baby care products, including baby diapers, wet wipes, and diaper pants, under private labels and own brands.
strengths: Expanding baby care consumption story in India
Strong contract manufacturing capabilities
Low-cost high-volume producer scale
risks: Heavy raw material cost volatility (fluff pulp/polymers)
High working capital requirement
Competition from established MNC brands

auditTrail.details: Imported during database migration

name: Millworks Technologies
about: Bengaluru-based AS9100D certified precision engineering firm specializing in high-precision machined components and assemblies for aerospace, defence, railways, and semiconductor sectors.
strengths: AS9100D aerospace & defence certification
Diverse sector exposure (aerospace, semiconductor, railways)
Strong financial growth with high ROE (69.94%)
risks: High customer concentration risk
Working capital intensive model
Raw material price volatility

name: Alpine Texworld
about: Ahmedabad-based textile manufacturer specializing in weaving and finishing of synthetic and cotton fabrics, launching its IPO to expand production capacity and repay debt.
strengths: Expanding weaving capacity with Unit 3
Consistent revenue growth (47% in FY26)
Established client relationship network
risks: High debt-to-equity ratio (2.35)
Highly competitive commodity textile market
Raw material (yarn) price sensitivity

name: Metalic Technoforge
leadManager: Smart Horizon Capital Advisors Pvt. Ltd.
about: Gujarat-based manufacturer of closed-die forged and precision-machined steel components for automotive, engineering, and industrial OEM sectors.
strengths: Strong post-IPO P/E valuation at 14.94x
Expanded Rajkot forging capacity with Unit IV
OEM and international supplier client relationships
risks: Highly working capital intensive model
Raw steel price volatility
Client concentration in automotive OEM sector

name: Shree Balaji (Mala) Textiles
about: Kolkata-based manufacturer and wholesale distributor of cotton sarees under the brand name 'Mala Saree', operating a manufacturing unit in Jetpur, Gujarat with a B2B network.
strengths: Established 'Mala Saree' brand name
Wide distribution network across India (3000+ retailers)
Manufacturing facility in Jetpur, Gujarat
risks: High debt levels relative to equity (D/E = 2.25)
Highly competitive wholesale textile market
Working capital intensive model

name: Xtranet Technologies
leadManager: Share India Capital Services Pvt. Ltd.
about: Bhopal-based integrated IT solutions provider offering ERP implementation, cloud virtualization, data center management, and customized application development.
strengths: Accelerating profitability (PAT up 174% in FY25)
Established presence in public sector/government IT
Broad product portfolio (ERP, Cloud, Virtualization)
risks: High working capital requirement
Government-client concentration risk
Competition from large global IT vendors

Experienced promoter group and management team
risks: Operating scale limits relative to larger peers
